summary: Threat-model walk over the Stages 1-9 per-actor channel architecture. One Medium and one Low, no High/Critical. Tests green (C# 2762/2762; PLang 205 pass + 6 fixture-fails). codeanalyzer v3 / tester v7 closures re-verified.

findings.title: Channel.Stream read path ignores Channel.Buffer; unbounded read
findings.responsibility: Stream channel core: bounded I/O over an arbitrary stream.
findings.blast_radius_today: stdin (local DoS / OOM via huge piped input).
findings.blast_radius_when_wired: Any future Stream-channel-backed HTTP body / socket / pipe ingest is unprotected — same shape as the runtime2-builder-v2-http response body cap.
findings.fix_posture: Enforce Channel.Buffer in ReadAllBytesAsync via chunked read with running-total cap; throw on exceed.

findings.title: MigrationEnvelope.Signature has PKI-shape fields but is a keyless integrity hash; doc/impl mismatch on coverage
issues: Bytes = SHA256(name|direction|identity) — no key; anyone can forge a verifying envelope.
Doc on Signature.Bytes claims coverage of (Name, Direction, Config, Payload); impl covers only (Name, Direction, IdentityName). Config and Payload are not even in the integrity hash.

Struct named Signature with IdentityName + PublicKey fields signals a PKI signature; future maintainer landing FromMigration could plausibly use VerifyEnvelope as the trust gate — that would be a complete bypass.
findings.today_mitigation: Migrate() returns Data; PlangDataSerializer egress lazy-signs the outer Data via EnsureSigned (real Ed25519). FromMigration throws NotImplementedException, so no consumer exists.
findings.fix_posture: Drop the misleading struct: rename inner field IntegrityHash, remove identity fields, delete VerifyEnvelope. Or wire through real Ed25519. Or fail-closed on Migrate() until receive-side ships.

findings.title: Channel.Goal.Migrate carries Variables.Snapshot() — standing Sensitive-leak finding applies
findings.context: Snapshot() doesn't honour [Sensitive] (existing standing finding in MEMORY.md). Wire serializers (JsonStreamSerializer, PlangDataSerializer) apply SensitivePropertyFilter.Strip recursively, which catches structured [Sensitive]-tagged properties (e.g. Identity.PrivateKey). Plaintext-string user variables remain unredacted (no attribute on raw strings). New use site of an existing tracked limitation; not a new finding.

findings.title: MigrationEnvelope.Payload is object? — polymorphic deserialize hazard for future receive side
findings.context: FromMigration is NotImplementedException today. When it ships, polymorphic deserialize via System.Text.Json on object? is unsafe. Receive-side must use a discriminator + closed [JsonDerivedType] set, or treat Payload as opaque bytes decoded after outer signature verifies.

findings.title: Channel.Stream.ResolveEncoding silently falls back to UTF-8 on unknown encoding name
findings.context: Operational misconfig, not a security boundary. Two peers configured with different encodings will produce mojibake silently. Logged warning on fallback would surface the mistake.

codeanalyzer_v3_B1_active_static_to_inst.evidence: PLang/App/Channels/Channel/Events/this.cs:22 — `private readonly` (no static). Probe test EventsActiveSet_IsInstanceScoped_NotShared added in coder v8.

codeanalyzer_v3_L1_enter_copy_on_write.evidence: PLang/App/Channels/Channel/Events/this.cs:69-85 — copy-on-write Enter, parent-restoring Releaser. Probe test Enter_FromConcurrentChild_DoesNotLeakChildIdToParentFlow added in coder v8.

codeanalyzer_v3_I1_variables_snapshot_ov.status: deferred (design call) — captured here as N1 for the new Goal-channel migrate use site

csharp.rebuild: clean (rm -rf bin obj across PlangConsole/PLang/PLang.Tests/PLang.Generators per CLAUDE.md stale-binary protocol)

next_step: Branch is ready to merge. F1 should land before any non-stdin Stream channel use ships. F2 cleanest fix (delete VerifyEnvelope, rename inner field) can land now while no callers exist.
next_bot_reason: Independent re-verification of F1 (Stream Buffer cap) and F2 (MigrationEnvelope misleading-signature) before any code change lands — same security→auditor→coder pattern used on runtime2-callback. After auditor passes, hand to coder for the F1 + F2 fixes. Fast-path: skip auditor and go straight to coder if the F2 cleanup feels uncontroversial — landing it now while no caller exists is safer than after merge.
